steps.title: Simplify the function
steps.content:
We have $c(r) = \frac{2}{5}(2\pi r)$. Multiplying the constants:
$$c(r) = \frac{4\pi r}{5}$$
This is a linear function with slope $\frac{4\pi}{5}$.

steps.title: Find k using the rate of change
steps.content:
For a linear function, when the radius increases by 18 cm, the change in $c$ is:
$$\Delta c = \frac{4\pi}{5} \cdot 18 = \frac{72\pi}{5}$$
Since this change equals $k\pi$, we have $k\pi = \frac{72\pi}{5}$, so $k = \frac{72}{5} = 14.4$.
